summary: Draft the complete book-concept deliverable package, run a completeness critic against the Definition of Done, fix gaps
logs: Critic: DoD NOT MET; 5 findings (1 blockers)

drafted: Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/01-executive-summary.md — a ~2-page executive summary leading with the honest two-number usage story and the corrected 1-in-4 hook, applying all 11 adjudication revisions (crowded-adjacent-shelf/empty-niche frame naming Turkle/Twenge/Suskind/AAP/Susskind/Muldoon, "ban just passed — now what?" primary frame, exit-ramp-to-zero thesis, SB 243-based leverage with settlements as narrative-only, migration-as-hypothesis, reset Q4 2026 schedule, Good Inside-lane P&L sizing), with every statistic inline-cited to corrected_facts source URLs, plus headline risks and a pointer map to documents 02-07.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/02-market-evidence.md — a six-section market evidence dossier (phenomenon with two-number framing, harm evidence with peer-review status and settlement caveats, parental demand, regulatory calendar with exact dates, audience math, and an explicit unverified/contested section), with every statistic cited inline to the adjudication's canonical sources.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/03-book-concept.md — the full post-adjudication book concept with all 11 concept revisions applied, corrected-fact stat wordings, inline source citations, the crowded-adjacent-shelf/empty-niche competitive frame naming all six adjacent titles plus Haidt as active incumbent, the SB 243-based playbook with the confidential no-admission settlement caveat, the exit-ramp-to-zero thesis reframe, the Track-B-primary Q4 2026 author timeline, and the anti-panic calibration stance with the 80% calibrating stat.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/04-outline.md: full front-matter-through-back-matter chapter outline (Start Here triage + 14 chapters in 4 parts + 5 appendices) with all 11 adjudication revisions applied — Chapter 9 rebuilt on SB 243's private right of action/crisis-protocol mandates/break reminders/disclosure duties with a non-California section and settlements demoted to confidential no-admission narrative why-now; triage and Breakup chapters explicitly citing and out-depthing the JED/APA free layer; migration framed as a commissioned-survey hypothesis; honest two-number usage framing and corrected 1-in-4 hook; Character.AI change scoped correctly; Turkle-wave 'crowded adjacent shelf, empty niche' positioning; Haidt treated as active incumbent; plus the four reader-facing artifacts and a word-count/structure spec, with every statistic cited to a real source URL.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/05-competitive-analysis.md — the full 'crowded adjacent shelf, empty niche' competitive analysis with all eight adjacent-wave titles, the free-content overlap analysis, the KDP layer, adjudication-compliant P&L sizing (Anxious Generation ceiling with corrected 111-week/2M+ figures, Good Inside-lane sizing), and the three-scenario preemption watch with tripwires, using only corrected_facts wordings and inline citations from the research base.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/06-pitch-materials.md: complete pitch package (elevator pitch, agent query letter, back-cover copy, one-page editor overview, 10 calendar-pegged marketing hooks with media formats, launch social/newsletter copy, and title decision with alternatives), using only the adjudication's corrected fact wordings with inline source links for every statistic.

/home/user/claude-code/book-concept/07-validation-and-risks.md is complete and verified: adversarial process summary (5 kill-angles, 28 attacks: 3 refuted/16 weakened/9 survived; 21 fact-checks: 15 confirmed/6 corrected), the three refuted claims with forced revisions, the full 17-entry corrected-facts table with canonical wordings and sources, the 10-item risk register verbatim-faithful, all 7 survived kill scenarios, the adjudicator's verdict quoted in full, and go/no-go gates (author gate, P&L sizing, legal review) — every URL cross-checked against the phase1/phase3/phase4 research files.

Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/08-methodology.md — a five-section methodology appendix covering the four-phase pipeline, the full tournament score table with content-matched loser sketches and Phase 1 discards, honest research-constraint disclosure (egress-proxy fetch blocks, the score-table rationale cross-attribution artifact, and a 10-point re-verification checklist), a topic-organized source inventory of all key URLs, and an AI-generation disclosure, using only adjudication-canonical fact wordings with inline citations.
result.readme: Wrote /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/README.md after reading all eight numbered documents (01-executive-summary through 08-methodology) and the research/ listing. The README covers: (1) what the package is — a complete, evidence-validated book concept built from open-internet market research in July 2026 via a four-phase multi-agent pipeline, presented post-adjudication; (2) a one-paragraph concept summary of *Your Kid's AI Best Friend* with the honest headline stats (72% tried under the broad CSM definition, ~1 in 8 / ~3M teens using chatbots for emotional support per Pew, 1 in 4 teens has at least once taken a serious problem to an AI, 18% parental comfort) and the deliberately narrow niche claim; (3) a reading-order table with one-line descriptions for docs 01-08; (4) an explanation of research/ as the raw machine-readable JSON pipeline outputs and audit trail, noting the documented tournament-file attribution artifact; (5) a seven-point Definition of Done (multi-scout convergence, tournament win, adversarial survival with all 11 revisions applied, canonical cited stat wordings, named-rival competitive frame, disclosed risks/constraints, archived raw outputs); and (6) the AI-generation and fact-reverification disclosure, pointing to 08-methodology §3 for the priority re-verification list. Kept to roughly one screen without restating the documents.
omissions: Companion-app market growth rate (18-31% CAGR) — mentioned in kill_scenarios_survived but with no source URL provided
Meta rebuilding teen AI characters — mentioned in kill_scenarios_survived without a source URL
The Tech Exit's ECPA-only sales performance as the follower-title base rate — referenced in adjudication without a citable source, so the P&L point was made via the Good Inside comp instead
CSM's ~13% teen daily-use figure — adjudication flags it as 'less firmly pinned,' so only the confirmed 52% regular-use figure was used

The AAP's Connecting (Moreno & Radesky) and Daniel Susskind's What Should My Children Do? are named per the mandatory competitive frame but left unlinked, as no catalog URL for either appears in the research files
Bark/Aura monitoring subscription pricing ($8-32/month) — figure appears only in the adjudication text with no source URL; vendors mentioned qualitatively instead
AI-companion market growth rate of 18-31% CAGR — asserted in adjudication kill-scenario notes without a source
Meta rebuilding teen AI characters — asserted in adjudication kill-scenario notes without a source

The Tech Exit's specific sales record (topping only Christian-retail/ECPA lists) — no source URL; referenced only generically as 'post-Haidt follower titles underperformed, per this project's adversarial demand review'

Specific outlets for settlement coverage beyond CNN/CNBC/WaPo/Bloomberg Law (CBS Mornings, ABC, Axios, Glenn Beck) — no URLs provided; retained only with explicit attribution to the project's fact-check rather than as independently cited fact

Common Sense Media teen-AI census described as 'now-annual' — no source URL confirming an annual cadence; softened to 'now-annual survey cycle' only in the forward-pegs list where it derives from the adjudication's confirmed regulatory-calendar fact

Przybylski/Bangor/Topoi moral-panic skeptic citations — the adjudication names them but provides no URLs, so the document references 'the moral-panic skeptics in the academic literature' generically without naming or linking them

The Tech Exit's ECPA-only sales performance — no source URL provided in any input, so the document says only that post-Haidt follower titles 'have underperformed the Haidt analogy' attributed to the package's internal market research, without the specific title or list claim
AI companion market 18-31% CAGR growth figure (from kill_scenarios_survived) — no source URL provided, omitted entirely
Laurie Segall's Mostly Human (mentioned in the risk register's adjacent-wave list) — no source URL or publication details provided, omitted from the competitive table

The FAccT 2026 corroborating paper — an ACM DOI was provided but the adjudication gives no descriptive detail safe to characterize, so only the CHI 2026 Drexel study is cited for the addiction-language finding
The '~13% daily use' secondary figure from the CSM survey — the adjudication flags it as 'less firmly pinned than the 52%', so the document uses only the FAU 11.4% near-daily figure for use intensity

Specific details of OpenAI's age-prediction system and Meta's teen parental controls (named in the adjudication's 'Why the Settings Aren't Enough' revision but with no source URL provided) — the section was kept but written generically about platform age-gates and parental-control dashboards
The '~13% daily use' secondary figure from CSM summaries — adjudication flags it as 'less firmly pinned,' so the outline uses only the 52% regular-use and FAU 11.4% near-daily figures
Przybylski/Bangor skeptic literature is named (as the adjudication requires) but with no inline citation, since no URL for that literature appears in any input file

AAP 'Connecting' (Moreno & Radesky) and Daniel Susskind's 'What Should My Children Do?' are named per the mandatory competitive frame but left unlinked — no catalog URL for either appears in the input files
Bark/Aura subscription price range ($8-32/month) — vendors named qualitatively as bundling/affiliate channels, but no source URL existed for the price figures

De Kai's Raising AI bestseller-list appearances and PRH paperback pickup — the adjudication's 'affirmative retail datapoint' had no supporting URL, so Raising AI is described only via its verifiable MIT Press/Amazon listing

Specific sales figures or NYT-week counts for Good Inside and How to Talk So Kids Will Listen — described qualitatively (category leadership, decades-long backlist) because no sourced numbers were in the research base
The Tech Exit's specific ECPA chart positions/sales numbers — its Christian-retail-only bestseller record is stated qualitatively per the adjudication, without figures or a URL

Lisa Damour's specific platform-building evidence (revised Untangled edition, May 2026 Bloomberg Opinion column) — mentioned only as 'actively developing her own teens-and-AI public presence' because no URLs were provided
UNICEF's parent guide — named in phase-1 evidence as part of the stopgap-guide flood but omitted from the free-content section for lack of a URL
AI-companion market growth rate (18-31% CAGR) — appeared in kill_scenarios_survived without a source URL
Laurie Segall's Mostly Human publisher/date details — named as a standing watch item only, since the risk register gave no bibliographic data or URL

Wait Until 8th pledge named as a distribution precedent — no source URL in the research files, so the school-newsletter viral mechanic is framed via Haidt's After Babel pledge machinery (which has a source) instead

Specific platform parental-control launches (OpenAI age prediction, Meta parental controls) — mentioned only generically in hook 9 because the adjudication text supplied no source URLs or dates for them
CSM's ~13% daily-use figure — flagged in corrected_facts as 'less firmly pinned,' so the 52% regular-use figure was used instead
Laurie Segall's Mostly Human (mentioned in the risk register) — no publisher/source URL provided, so it is not named in the competitive frame
Good Inside and Lisa Damour comp sales figures — no source URLs in the inputs, so both are referenced by title/pattern only with no numbers

AAP Connecting (Moreno & Radesky) and Daniel Susskind's What Should My Children Do? — no dedicated catalog URLs in the inputs, so both are cited via the Publishers Weekly May 4, 2026 wave roundup link
Companion-market growth CAGR (18-31%) from kill_scenarios_survived — no source URL provided, omitted entirely
None — every statistic and claim in the document carries a source from the adjudication's corrected_facts, the phase1 market research, or the phase3 adversarial file; no fact had to be dropped.

Deliberately excluded as unciteable per the adjudication (not omissions but mandated exclusions): any migration/displacement finding after the Nov 2025 Character.AI restriction (no published data — worded as hypothesis only), and any settlement-entitlement claim (settlements are confidential, no-admission).

Catalog/announcement URLs for the AAP's Connecting (Moreno & Radesky, Oct 2026) and Daniel Susskind's What Should My Children Do? (Penguin Press, Nov 2026) — both named per instructions in the competitive frame but left unlinked because no URL for either appears in the research files
The Tech Exit's ECPA-only/Christian-retail sales performance (cited by the demand skeptic with no source URL)
Bark/Aura monitoring-subscription pricing of $8-32/month (adjudication text only, no URL)
AI-companion market growth of 18-31% CAGR and Meta rebuilding teen AI characters (kill-scenario text only, no URLs)

Laurie Segall's Mostly Human, Lisa Damour's revised Untangled and May 2026 Bloomberg Opinion piece, and the Przybylski/Bangor/Topoi moral-panic-skeptic literature (all referenced in adjudication prose without source URLs)
OpenAI age-prediction and Meta parental-control feature details (concept-revision text only, no URLs)
Specific Idaho/Oregon/Washington/Nebraska statute citations — covered only generically via the MultiState overview link
Internet Matters (UK) triangulation data mentioned in the refined concept (no URL in any research file)
IC3/Vectra dollar-loss specifics for the scam-defense loser beyond the flagged SQ Magazine aggregate (FBI primary-source URL absent from the files)

critique.overall: The package is substantively excellent and highly faithful to the adjudication canon: every corrected stat I checked (the 72% try-rate qualification, the one-in-four/users-base fix, the settlement no-entitlement caveat, the Character.AI scope, the SB 243 provisions, the Haidt-incumbent reframe, the ~42M derived base) appears in its canonical wording with inline citations in all nine files; 07's counts (28 attacks with per-angle refuted/weakened/survived splits, 21 fact checks at 15 confirmed/6 partly-true, 11 revisions, 17 facts, 10 risks, 7 kill scenarios) match the raw phase3/phase4 JSON exactly; the tournament score table sums correctly; no placeholder text, no 'empty shelf' claims, no uncorrected inflated stats survive; disclosure and re-verification guidance are strong. However, parallel drafting left cross-document contradictions that break the 'stranger can evaluate without external help' bar: the market-ready pitch document (06) recommends the exact 'out-relate the machine' subtitle that 03 says was retired per the adjudicated thesis correction (with 01 carrying a third subtitle variant); 01's closing package map and 05's 'document 5 of 7' header describe a stale 7-document structure that misdirects a reader through the actual files; and the book's stated length differs across 03 (75–85k), 04 (82–90k/~90k), and 06 (~75k). All are quick, mechanical fixes; until they are made, a stranger encounters unresolvable conflicts on the title page and the basic spec of the product being pitched.

findings.issue: Subtitle contradiction with the adjudicated title decision: 06 recommends the subtitle '...Scripts, House Rules, and How to Out-Relate the Machine' (line 62 One-Page Overview, line 157 recommended title, and line 159 praises 'out-relate the machine' as 'the thesis verb the whole book conjugates'), but 03-book-concept.md §1 (line 12) explicitly states the subtitle 'has been revised in this document to retire the earlier "out-relate the machine" phrasing as the headline promise' because 'the subtitle must never be readable as "learn to live with your kid's AI girlfriend"' — the reasoning that flows from adjudication concept_revisions #6 (exit-ramp-to-zero thesis). 03's working subtitle is '...Scripts, House Rules, and the Way Out'; 01-executive-summary.md line 4 uses a third variant ('...and the Exit Ramp From a Teen's AI Companion'). The market-ready pitch document thus carries the exact phrasing another package document says was retired for cause.
findings.fix_instruction: Pick one canonical subtitle consistent with 03's stated rationale (e.g. 'What to Do When the Phone Loves Them Back — Scripts, House Rules, and the Way Out'). In 06-pitch-materials.md: replace the subtitle in line 62 (TITLE field) and line 157 (recommended title heading), and rewrite the 'Why it wins' paragraph (line 159) so it no longer praises 'out-relate the machine' as the subtitle's thesis verb — instead note the deliverables-first subtitle and that the exit-ramp thesis lives in the copy. In 01-executive-summary.md line 4, change the subtitle to the same canonical form. Leave the 'Out-Relate the Machine' entry in 06's alternatives table (line 168) as a rejected alternate, which is consistent.

findings.issue: The closing 'The rest of the package' paragraph (line 42) describes a stale 7-document package whose numbering and titles do not match the actual files: it calls 02 'Full Concept & Chapter Plan', 03 'Market & Audience Evidence', 04 'Competitive Landscape', 05 'Adversarial Review & Risk Register', 06 'Author, Positioning & Launch Plan', 07 'Methodology'. Actual files are 02-market-evidence, 03-book-concept, 04-outline, 05-competitive-analysis, 06-pitch-materials, 07-validation-and-risks, 08-methodology. A stranger following these pointers opens the wrong documents, and one promised document ('Author, Positioning & Launch Plan') does not exist under that name (author strategy is in 03 §10; launch/marketing is in 06).
findings.fix_instruction: Rewrite line 42 of 01-executive-summary.md to map to the real files: 02 — Market Evidence Dossier; 03 — The Book Concept (full positioning, title, thesis, author strategy); 04 — Chapter Outline; 05 — Competitive Analysis; 06 — Pitch Materials (query letter, back-cover copy, launch hooks); 07 — Validation Report and Risk Register (11 revisions, 17 corrected facts, 10 risks, kill scenarios); 08 — Methodology Appendix. Match the README reading-order table wording.

findings.issue: Word-count contradiction across the package: 06 states 'approx. 75,000 words' in both the agent query letter (line 25) and the One-Page Overview (line 64); 03-book-concept.md line 26 states 'Length: 75,000–85,000 words'; 04-outline.md line 7 states 'Target length: 82,000–90,000 words total' and its specification table (line 178) totals ~90,000. An acquiring editor comparing the pitch, the concept doc, and the outline gets three different length specs for the same book.
findings.fix_instruction: Harmonize to one spec. Simplest consistent fix: treat 04's detailed spec as canonical (~74,000 words of chapter text, ~90,000 total with front/back matter), then update 03 line 26 to that range and update 06 lines 25 and 64 to 'approx. 90,000 words' (or 'approx. 75,000 words of chapter text plus triage front section and appendices, ~90,000 total'). Alternatively shrink 04's per-chapter targets to fit 75,000–85,000 — but all three documents must state the same number.

findings.issue: Header (line 5) reads 'Package document 5 of 7' — the package contains 8 numbered documents plus a README, so the '(of 7)' count is wrong and reflects the same stale 7-document plan found in 01 line 42. A stranger cross-checking package completeness would conclude a document is missing or extra.
findings.fix_instruction: In 05-competitive-analysis.md line 5, change 'Package document 5 of 7' to 'Package document 5 of 8' (or simply 'Package document 5 | July 2026').

findings.issue: Internal arithmetic inconsistency in the length spec: line 7 says 'approx. 72,000 words of chapter text' but the document's own Word-Count and Structure Specification sums chapter text to 74,000 (Part I 21,500 + Part II 16,500 + Part III 22,000 + Part IV 14,000); the header's low bound '82,000' is also inconsistent with the spec table, which totals exactly ~90,000 with no stated variance mechanism.
findings.fix_instruction: In 04-outline.md line 7, change 'approx. 72,000 words of chapter text' to 'approx. 74,000 words of chapter text' and either state a single ~90,000 total or explain what the 82,000 floor represents (e.g. '±10% per-chapter variance'). Keep whichever total is adopted for the package-wide fix in finding 3.

result.fix_report: {"fixed":["Finding 1 (blocker, subtitle contradiction): Adopted 03's canonical subtitle '...Scripts, House Rules, and the Way Out' everywhere. /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/06-pitch-materials.md line 62 (TITLE field) and line 157 (recommended-title heading) now use the canonical subtitle; the 'Why it wins' paragraph (line 159) no longer praises 'out-relate the machine' as the thesis verb — it now notes the deliverables-first subtitle, that 'the way out' promises the exit ramp without being readable as 'learn to live with your kid's AI girlfriend', and that the exit-ramp thesis lives in the copy. /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/01-executive-summary.md line 4 changed from the '...Exit Ramp From a Teen's AI Companion' variant to the canonical subtitle. The 'Out-Relate the Machine' row in 06's alternatives table was left as a rejected alternate, per instructions.","Finding 2 (major, stale package map): Rewrote the 'The rest of the package' paragraph in /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/01-executive-summary.md (line 42) to map to the real eight files with README-consistent wording: 02 Market Evidence, 03 The Book Concept, 04 Chapter Outline, 05 Competitive Analysis, 06 Pitch Materials, 07 Validation and Risks (eleven revisions, seventeen corrected facts, ten risks, kill scenarios), 08 Methodology.","Finding 3 (major, cross-package word-count contradiction): Harmonized on 04's detailed spec as canonical (~74,000 words chapter text, ~90,000 total). /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/06-pitch-materials.md line 25 (query letter) now reads 'approx. 90,000 words, of which about 74,000 are chapter text' and line 64 (One-Page Overview) 'Approx. 90,000 words (about 74,000 words of chapter text plus triage front section and appendices)'. /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/03-book-concept.md line 26 now reads 'approx. 90,000 words total (about 74,000 words of chapter text plus front and back matter; full specification in the chapter outline, document 04)'.","Finding 4 (minor, stale '(of 7)' header): /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/05-competitive-analysis.md line 5 changed to 'Package document 5 of 8 | July 2026'.","Finding 5 (minor, internal arithmetic in outline): /home/user/claude-code/book-concept/04-outline.md line 7 changed to 'approx. 90,000 words total (approx. 74,000 words of chapter text plus front and back matter, with ±10% per-chapter variance...)' — matching its own spec table, which sums exactly to 90,000 (chapter text 21,500+16,500+22,000+14,000 = 74,000); the unexplained 82,000 floor and the wrong 72,000 figure are removed."],"could_not_fix":[]}
